preview_text:
The model rates Argentina as a clear favorite at 76%, leaving Austria just 4% to claim victory. The underdog's path is narrow, though the draw at 20% offers a secondary outcome where Austria avoids defeat.

The win-loss split reflects substantial daylight between the teams in the model's assessment. Austria's 4% win probability sits far below both draw and loss scenarios, suggesting the model sees this as unlikely to be competitive at the decisive end. A draw remains plausible at one-in-five odds, but Argentina's dominance in the forecast is pronounced.

Model and market diverge meaningfully here. The market consensus assigns Argentina only 58–60%, roughly 16–18 percentage points lower than the model, while pricing Austria's chances at 17–18% — more than four times the model estimate. Polymarket and sportsbooks align closely with each other. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct; the gap warrants attention from users comparing computational and market views.
